hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will improve SSTR2 selectivity by forming favorable interactions with SSTR2-specific ECL2 residues (192/193/195/197), while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will introduce repulsive interactions with off-target SSTR3/SSTR5, thereby increasing the selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating candidates with strong ddG but insufficient structural quality and selectivity. Focusing optimization on non-pharmacophore positions and tightening structural filters will improve both yield and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Reducing the clash tolerance will enforce better packing and reduce structural failures due to steric clashes, especially in the binding pocket.

Focus optimization on non-pharmacophore positions known to contribute to off-target binding (SSTR3/SSTR5). This will help disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14).
